1	# SCRATCH.md -- Comprehensive Codebase Reference
2	
3	## 1. Project Overview
4	
5	**What:** A real-time collaborative whiteboard. Multiple users share a canvas, draw shapes, see each other's cursors, and persist state across sessions.
6	
7	**Deployment context:** 2-5 people on a video call doing synchronous design reviews. Sessions run 30-90 minutes. Teams revisit canvases later. Concurrent same-object edits are common.
8	
9	**Origin:** Mechanize take-home interview assignment (3-hour solo block, followed by a defense call).
10	
11	**Tech Stack (locked):**
12	
13	| Layer       | Technology                                   |
14	|-------------|----------------------------------------------|
15	| Frontend    | React 19, TypeScript 5.6, Vite 6             |
16	| Backend     | FastAPI (Starlette), Python 3.12, Uvicorn    |
17	| Database    | PostgreSQL 16                                |
18	| DB driver   | asyncpg (async, `$1` param syntax)           |
19	| Auth        | JWT (HS256) via PyJWT, bcrypt password hashes |
20	| Transport   | Single WebSocket per user per canvas + HTTP for auth/CRUD |
21	| Containers  | Docker Compose (db, server, client)          |
22	
23	---
24	
25	## 2. Architecture Diagram
26	
27	```
28	+---------------------------+       +----------------------------+
29	|        Browser (React)    |       |        Browser (React)     |
30	|                           |       |                            |
31	|  Auth -> localStorage     |       |  Auth -> localStorage      |
32	|  CanvasPage -> <canvas>   |       |  CanvasPage -> <canvas>    |
33	|                           |       |                            |
34	|  HTTP: /api/auth/*        |       |  HTTP: /api/auth/*         |
35	|  HTTP: /api/canvases/*    |       |  HTTP: /api/canvases/*     |
36	|  WS:   /ws?canvasId&token |       |  WS:   /ws?canvasId&token  |
37	+----------+-------+--------+       +--------+--------+----------+
38	           |       |                          |        |
39	     HTTP  |       | WS                 HTTP  |        | WS
40	           |       |                          |        |
41	+----------v-------v--------------------------v--------v----------+
42	|                    FastAPI Server (:3001)                        |
43	|                                                                 |
44	|  main.py          -- lifespan, CORS, error handler, route wiring|
45	|  auth.py          -- POST /signup, /login, GET /me              |
46	|  canvas.py        -- GET/POST /canvases, GET /:id, POST /invite |
47	|  ws.py            -- WS /ws: auth, init, ops, cursors, presence |
48	|  deps.py          -- get_current_user (JWT decode from header)  |
49	|  db.py            -- asyncpg pool singleton                     |
50	|  types.py         -- shared TypedDicts (mirror of types.ts)     |
51	|                                                                 |
52	|  In-memory state (ws.py module globals):                        |
53	|    presence{}     -- canvas_id -> user_id -> {ws, color, ...}   |
54	|    seq_counters{} -- canvas_id -> int (monotonic)               |
55	|    canvas_locks{} -- canvas_id -> asyncio.Lock                  |
56	+--------------------------+--------------------------------------+
57	                           |
58	                           | asyncpg (TCP :5432)
59	                           |
60	            +--------------v--------------+
61	            |     PostgreSQL 16 (:5432)   |
62	            |                             |
63	            |  users                      |
64	            |  canvases                   |
65	            |  canvas_members             |
66	            |  shapes                     |
67	            +-----------------------------+
68	
69	Data flow summary:
70	  HTTP: auth + canvas CRUD (JSON over fetch)
71	  WS:   ops + cursors + presence (JSON over single socket per user per canvas)
72	  DB:   shapes are mutated per-op (no op log); presence is memory-only
73	```
74	
75	---
76	
77	## 3. File Map
78	
79	### Root
80	
81	| File | Purpose |
82	|------|---------|
83	| `BUILD_CONTRACT.md` | Frozen contract: schemas, protocols, ownership map, dispatch prompts |
84	| `CLAUDE.md` | AI agent instructions: stack, conventions, gotchas, CVEs |
85	| `AGENTS.md` | Multi-agent ownership boundaries, handoff format, review checklist |
86	| `README.md` | Take-home assignment instructions and evaluation criteria |
87	| `SETUP.md` | How to run (Docker Compose and local dev) |
88	| `docker-compose.yml` | Three services: db (PG 16), server (FastAPI), client (Vite) |
89	| `package.json` | Monorepo root; npm workspaces pointing to `client/` |
90	| `.gitignore` | Excludes node_modules, dist, .env, __pycache__, .venv |
91	
92	### Server
93	
94	| File | Purpose |
95	|------|---------|
96	| `server/schema.sql` | DDL: users, canvases, canvas_members, shapes tables + indexes |
97	| `server/pyproject.toml` | Python project: fastapi, uvicorn, asyncpg, pyjwt, bcrypt |
98	| `server/Dockerfile` | Multi-stage build: compile C extensions in builder, slim runtime |
99	| `server/app/__init__.py` | Empty package marker |
100	| `server/app/main.py` | FastAPI app: lifespan (pool init/close), CORS, error handler, route wiring, `/health` |
101	| `server/app/db.py` | asyncpg pool singleton (`get_pool()`, `close_pool()`) |
102	| `server/app/deps.py` | `get_current_user()`: JWT decode from Authorization header, returns `AuthUser` |
103	| `server/app/types.py` | Python TypedDicts mirroring `types.ts`: Shape, Operation, all messages, mappings |
104	| `server/app/auth.py` | `auth_router`: POST /signup, POST /login, GET /me |
105	| `server/app/canvas.py` | `canvas_router`: GET /, POST /, GET /{id}, POST /{id}/invite |
106	| `server/app/ws.py` | `websocket_endpoint()`: auth, init, op persist+broadcast, cursor relay, presence |
107	
108	### Client
109	
110	| File | Purpose |
111	|------|---------|
112	| `client/package.json` | React 19, Vite 6, TypeScript 5.6 dependencies |
113	| `client/tsconfig.json` | Strict mode, ES2022 target, react-jsx, bundler resolution |
114	| `client/vite.config.ts` | Dev proxy: `/api` -> `:3001`, `/ws` -> `ws://:3001` |
115	| `client/index.html` | SPA shell: `<div id="root">`, loads `/src/main.tsx` |
116	| `client/Dockerfile` | Node 22 Alpine, `npm install`, `npm run dev` |
117	| `client/src/types.ts` | CANONICAL shared contract: Shape, Operation, all message types |
118	| `client/src/main.tsx` | React 19 entry: StrictMode + createRoot |
119	| `client/src/App.tsx` | Root component: client-side routing via `Page` discriminated union |
120	| `client/src/api.ts` | HTTP client: typed fetch wrappers for all API endpoints |
121	| `client/src/authStore.ts` | localStorage-based token/user persistence |
122	| `client/src/operations.ts` | `applyOp()`: applies Operation to Shape[]; `reverseOp()`: builds undo inverse |
123	| `client/src/canvasRenderer.ts` | Canvas 2D rendering: shapes, selection box, handles, cursors, hit testing |
124	| `client/src/components/Auth.tsx` | Login/signup form with error handling |
125	| `client/src/components/CanvasList.tsx` | Lists user's canvases, create new canvas |
126	| `client/src/components/CanvasPage.tsx` | Main whiteboard: WS connection, mouse/keyboard handlers, undo/redo, rendering loop |
127	| `client/src/components/Toolbar.tsx` | Tool buttons, color pickers, undo/redo buttons |
128	| `client/src/components/InvitePanel.tsx` | Expandable panel: online users list, invite by username/email |
129	
130	---
131	
132	## 4. Data Flow
133	
134	### 4a. Auth Flow
135	
136	```
137	User fills signup form
138	  -> Auth.tsx handleSubmit()
139	    -> api.ts signup({ username, email, password })
140	      -> POST /api/auth/signup (JSON body)
141	        -> auth.py: hash password with bcrypt, INSERT INTO users
142	        -> Return { token: JWT, user: AuthUser }
143	      <- api.ts returns AuthResponse
144	    -> authStore.ts storeAuth(token, user) -- saves to localStorage
145	    -> App.tsx handleAuth() -- setPage({ kind: "canvases" })
146	
147	Subsequent requests:
148	  -> api.ts authHeaders() reads token from localStorage
149	  -> Sends Authorization: Bearer <token>
150	  -> deps.py get_current_user() decodes JWT, returns AuthUser
151	  -> If expired/invalid: 401 -> ApiError -> redirect to login
152	
153	JWT payload: { sub: userId, username, email, exp: now + 24h }
154	Signed with JWT_SECRET (HS256)
155	```
156	
157	### 4b. Canvas CRUD Flow
158	
159	```
160	CanvasList mounts
161	  -> useEffect calls api.getCanvases()
162	    -> GET /api/canvases (authed)
163	      -> canvas.py: SELECT canvases JOIN canvas_members WHERE user_id = $1
164	      -> Returns CanvasSummary[]
165	
166	User creates canvas:
167	  -> handleCreate() -> api.createCanvas({ name })
168	    -> POST /api/canvases (authed)
169	      -> canvas.py: INSERT canvases + INSERT canvas_members (transaction)
170	      -> Returns CanvasSummary
171	
172	User clicks canvas:
173	  -> onSelectCanvas(canvasId) -> App sets page to { kind: "canvas", canvasId }
174	  -> CanvasPage mounts -> loads detail + connects WS (see 4c)
175	
176	User invites:
177	  -> InvitePanel handleInvite()
178	    -> api.inviteToCanvas(canvasId, { identifier })
179	      -> POST /api/canvases/{id}/invite
180	        -> canvas.py: look up user by username, then email
181	        -> INSERT canvas_members ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
182	        -> Returns { ok: true }
183	```
184	
185	### 4c. Drawing Flow (shape creation)
186	
187	```
188	User selects Rectangle tool, mousedown on canvas
189	  -> handleMouseDown(): creates previewShape with crypto.randomUUID()
190	  -> dragModeRef = { kind: "draw", startX, startY, previewShape }
191	
192	User drags mouse
193	  -> handleMouseMove(): updates previewShape dimensions
194	  -> requestRender() -> renderScene() draws preview on canvas
195	
196	User releases mouse
197	  -> handleMouseUp():
198	    -> if shape has size > 2px:
199	      -> Build forward = { kind: "add", shape }
200	      -> Build reverse = { kind: "delete", shapeId }
201	      -> doAction(forward, reverse):
202	        -> Push { forward, reverse } onto undoStack, clear redoStack
203	        -> sendOp(forward):
204	          -> Apply optimistically: shapesRef.current = applyOp(shapes, op)
205	          -> Generate opId = crypto.randomUUID()
206	          -> Add opId to pendingOps
207	          -> ws.send({ type: "op", op, opId })
208	
209	Server receives op (ws.py):
210	  -> Acquire canvas_locks[canvasId]
211	  -> _persist_add(): INSERT INTO shapes ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
212	  -> If inserted (modified=True):
213	    -> Increment seq_counters[canvasId]
214	    -> Release lock
215	    -> _broadcast_all(): send { type: "op", op, userId, seq, opId } to ALL clients
216	
217	Sender receives echo:
218	  -> ws.onmessage "op":
219	    -> pendingOps.has(opId) = true -> delete it
220	    -> op.kind === "add" -> skip (already applied optimistically)
221	
222	Other client receives op:
223	  -> pendingOps.has(opId) = false
224	    -> applyOp(shapes, op) -> shape added to their shapesRef
225	    -> requestRender() -> shape appears on their canvas
226	```
227	
228	### 4d. Undo/Redo Flow
229	
230	```
231	Undo (Ctrl+Z):
232	  -> undo():
233	    -> Pop last entry from undoStack
234	    -> Push to redoStack
235	    -> sendOp(entry.reverse):
236	      -> Apply reverse op optimistically
237	      -> Send as normal op over WS
238	  -> Server persists the reverse op (e.g., DELETE for an add's undo)
239	  -> Server broadcasts to all clients
240	
241	Redo (Ctrl+Shift+Z):
242	  -> redo():
243	    -> Pop last entry from redoStack
244	    -> Push to undoStack
245	    -> sendOp(entry.forward)
246	  -> Same persist + broadcast cycle
247	
248	Reverse op construction (operations.ts reverseOp()):
249	  - add -> delete (shapeId)
250	  - delete -> add (snapshot of shape before deletion)
251	  - update -> update (only changed fields' old values)
252	  - Returns null if shape not found (already deleted by another user)
253	
254	Key: server sees undo/redo as normal ops. No special undo protocol.
255	```
256	
257	### 4e. Cursor Presence Flow
258	
259	```
260	User moves mouse on canvas
261	  -> handleMouseMove():
262	    -> Throttle check: Date.now() - lastCursorSendRef >= 30ms
263	    -> sendWs({ type: "cursor", x, y })
264	
265	Server receives cursor (ws.py):
266	  -> _broadcast() to all clients EXCEPT sender:
267	    -> { type: "cursor", userId, username, x, y }
268	
269	Other client receives cursor:
270	  -> ws.onmessage "cursor":
271	    -> cursorsRef.current.set(userId, { x, y, username, color })
272	    -> requestRender()
273	    -> canvasRenderer.ts drawCursor(): arrow + username label
274	
275	User connects:
276	  -> Server adds to presence[canvasId][userId]
277	  -> Assigns color from CURSOR_PALETTE (round-robin by current count)
278	  -> Sends "init" with all current users
279	  -> Broadcasts "join" to others
280	
281	User disconnects:
282	  -> Server removes from presence
283	  -> Broadcasts "leave" to remaining clients
284	  -> Client removes cursor from map on "leave" message
285	```
286	
287	---
288	
289	## 5. Database Schema
290	
291	```sql
292	-- pgcrypto enables gen_random_uuid()
293	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
294	
295	CREATE TABLE users (
296	    id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
297	    username      TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
298	    email         TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
299	    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
300	    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
301	);
302	
303	CREATE TABLE canvases (
304	    id         UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
305	    name       TEXT NOT NULL,
306	    owner_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
307	    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
308	);
309	
310	CREATE TABLE canvas_members (
311	    canvas_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES canvases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
312	    user_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
313	    PRIMARY KEY (canvas_id, user_id)
314	);
315	
316	CREATE TABLE shapes (
317	    id           UUID PRIMARY KEY,              -- client-generated, no default
318	    canvas_id    UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES canvases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
319	    type         TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- rectangle|ellipse|line|text
320	    x            DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
321	    y            DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
322	    width        DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
323	    height       DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
324	    fill         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
325	    stroke       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '#000000',
326	    stroke_width DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 2,
327	    text         TEXT,                           -- nullable, for text shapes
328	    font_size    DOUBLE PRECISION                -- nullable, for text shapes
329	);
330	
331	CREATE INDEX idx_shapes_canvas ON shapes(canvas_id);
332	CREATE INDEX idx_canvas_members_user ON canvas_members(user_id);
333	```
334	
335	**Relationships:**
336	- `canvases.owner_id` -> `users.id` (no cascade -- owner deletion not handled)
337	- `canvas_members` is a join table: composite PK `(canvas_id, user_id)`, both FK with CASCADE
338	- `shapes.canvas_id` -> `canvases.id` ON DELETE CASCADE
339	
340	**Column naming:**
341	- DB uses `snake_case`: `stroke_width`, `font_size`, `owner_id`, `created_at`
342	- Wire format uses `camelCase`: `strokeWidth`, `fontSize`, `ownerId`, `createdAt`
343	- Mapping defined in `types.py`: `SHAPE_DB_TO_WIRE` / `SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB`
344	
345	---
346	
347	## 6. WebSocket Protocol
348	
349	### Connection
350	
351	```
352	WS /ws?canvasId=<uuid>&token=<jwt>
353	```
354	
355	Server validates JWT and canvas membership BEFORE `ws.accept()`.
356	- Close `4001`: auth failure (bad/missing token, bad canvasId)
357	- Close `4003`: not a canvas member
358	
359	### Client -> Server
360	
361	| Type | Fields | Description |
362	|------|--------|-------------|
363	| `op` | `type: "op"`, `op: Operation`, `opId: string` | Board operation (add/update/delete) with client-generated UUID |
364	| `cursor` | `type: "cursor"`, `x: number`, `y: number` | Cursor position in canvas coordinates |
365	
366	`Operation` is one of:
367	- `{ kind: "add", shape: Shape }` -- full Shape object with client-generated id
368	- `{ kind: "update", shapeId: string, props: ShapePatch }` -- partial field update
369	- `{ kind: "delete", shapeId: string }` -- remove by id
370	
371	### Server -> Client
372	
373	| Type | Fields | Description |
374	|------|--------|-------------|
375	| `init` | `type`, `shapes: Shape[]`, `users: PresenceUser[]`, `seq: number` | Sent on connect. Full current state. |
376	| `op` | `type`, `op: Operation`, `userId: string`, `seq: number`, `opId: string` | Persisted op echoed to ALL clients (including sender) |
377	| `cursor` | `type`, `userId: string`, `username: string`, `x: number`, `y: number` | Cursor position from another user (never sent to self) |
378	| `join` | `type`, `user: PresenceUser` | New user connected (sent to others) |
379	| `leave` | `type`, `userId: string` | User disconnected (sent to others) |
380	| `error` | `type`, `message: string` | Error message |
381	
382	`PresenceUser`: `{ userId, username, color }`
383	
384	### Server Op Processing (steps from contract)
385	
386	1. Receive `{ type: "op", op, opId }`
387	2. Acquire per-canvas `asyncio.Lock`
388	3. Persist to DB (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)
389	4. If no-op (row not modified): release lock, stop. No broadcast, no seq.
390	5. Increment `seq_counters[canvasId]`
391	6. Release lock
392	7. Broadcast `{ type: "op", op, userId, seq, opId }` to all clients
393	
394	---
395	
396	## 7. Key Design Decisions
397	
398	### 7a. Server-Authoritative LWW with Seq Numbers
399	
400	The server is the single source of truth. There is no OT or CRDT. Every successful mutation gets a per-canvas monotonic `seq` number. Clients track `seqRef` but currently don't use it for ordering -- they apply ops as they arrive. `seq` primarily exists to detect how far behind a client is and to enable future reconnection logic.
401	
402	Conflict resolution is **last-write-wins at field level by server arrival order**. If User A sets `fill: red` and User B sets `fill: blue`, whichever the server processes second wins. Because `ShapePatch` is partial, different-field edits (e.g., A changes fill, B changes x) never conflict.
403	
404	### 7b. Optimistic Updates with opId Reconciliation
405	
406	```typescript
407	// CanvasPage.tsx sendOp():
408	const opId = crypto.randomUUID();
409	shapesRef.current = applyOp(shapesRef.current, op);  // optimistic
410	pendingOpsRef.current.add(opId);
411	ws.send({ type: "op", op, opId });
412	```
413	
414	When the server echo comes back:
415	
416	```typescript
417	// ws.onmessage "op":
418	const isOwnEcho = pendingOpsRef.current.has(msg.opId);
419	if (isOwnEcho) pendingOpsRef.current.delete(msg.opId);
420	
421	if (isOwnEcho && msg.op.kind === "add") {
422	  // Skip -- shape already in array from optimistic add
423	} else {
424	  shapesRef.current = applyOp(shapesRef.current, msg.op);
425	  // Re-applies update/delete even for own ops
426	}
427	```
428	
429	**Why update/delete echoes are re-applied for own ops:** Under concurrent edits, the optimistic local state may have been overwritten by a remote op that arrived between the local apply and the server echo. Re-applying the server-authoritative version ensures convergence. For `add`, re-applying would create a duplicate, so it's skipped.
430	
431	If the server ignores a no-op (e.g., deleting an already-deleted shape), the pending opId is never removed. The optimistic state diverges until the next `init` on reconnect corrects it. This is an acceptable tradeoff given the deployment context.
432	
433	### 7c. Per-Canvas asyncio.Lock for Op Serialization
434	
435	```python
436	# ws.py
437	canvas_locks: dict[str, asyncio.Lock] = defaultdict(asyncio.Lock)
438	
439	async with lock:
440	    async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
441	        modified = await _persist_add(conn, canvas_id, shape)
442	    if modified:
443	        seq_counters[canvas_id] += 1
444	        seq = seq_counters[canvas_id]
445	# Lock released BEFORE broadcast
446	```
447	
448	One lock per canvas ensures ops for the same canvas are serialized -- no interleaved reads/writes to shapes, no seq races. Different canvases are fully parallel. The lock is released before the broadcast to avoid holding it during potentially slow network I/O.
449	
450	### 7d. Client-Side Undo/Redo with Field-Level Reverse Ops
451	
452	```typescript
453	// operations.ts reverseOp():
454	case "update": {
455	  const reverseProps: ShapePatch = {};
456	  for (const key of Object.keys(op.props)) {
457	    reverseProps[key] = shape[key];  // capture old value of ONLY changed fields
458	  }
459	  return { kind: "update", shapeId, props: reverseProps };
460	}
461	```
462	
463	Undo/redo are purely client-side stacks. The server never sees "undo" as a concept -- it receives the reverse operation as a normal op. This means:
464	
465	- Different-field concurrent edits survive undo (field-level capture, not snapshot)
466	- Undo of "add" sends "delete" -- even if others modified the shape
467	- Undo of "delete" sends "add" with a pre-delete snapshot -- later concurrent changes are lost
468	- If the target shape no longer exists, the server ignores the op (no-op)
469	- `reverseOp()` returns `null` if the shape is already gone, and the caller skips pushing to the undo stack
470	
471	### 7e. Refs vs useState Split in React
472	
473	CanvasPage uses two categories of state:
474	
475	**Refs** (high-frequency, no re-render needed):
476	- `shapesRef` -- shapes array (updated 60fps during drag, on every WS op)
477	- `cursorsRef` -- remote cursor positions (updated on every WS cursor message)
478	- `dragModeRef` -- current drag interaction state
479	- `selectedIdRef` -- currently selected shape
480	- `pendingOpsRef` -- Set of opIds awaiting server echo
481	- `undoStackRef` / `redoStackRef` -- undo stacks
482	- `seqRef` -- latest server seq
483	- `wsRef` -- WebSocket instance
484	- `lastCursorSendRef` -- throttle timestamp
485	
486	**useState** (triggers re-renders for UI):
487	- `currentTool`, `fillColor`, `strokeColor` -- toolbar state
488	- `undoCount`, `redoCount` -- enables/disables undo/redo buttons
489	- `onlineUsers` -- user list in invite panel
490	- `wsConnected` -- connection indicator
491	- `error` -- error banner
492	
493	**Why:** React 19 batches setState calls, and rapid WS messages would either cause stale closures or dropped updates if shapes were in useState. By keeping shapes in a ref and using `requestAnimationFrame` for rendering, the canvas bypasses React's reconciliation entirely. The canvas 2D context is imperatively drawn, not declaratively rendered.
494	
495	### 7f. Per-Connection Send Locks (Frame Interleaving Prevention)
496	
497	```python
498	# ws.py
499	async def _safe_send(ws, data, send_lock):
500	    try:
501	        async with send_lock:
502	            await ws.send_text(data)
503	    except Exception:
504	        pass  # connection closed; cleanup happens in finally block
505	
506	# Each connection gets its own lock:
507	send_lock = asyncio.Lock()
508	canvas_presence[user_id] = { "ws": ws, "send_lock": send_lock, ... }
509	```
510	
511	Starlette does NOT serialize concurrent `send_text()` calls on the same WebSocket. If two async tasks broadcast simultaneously, their frames can interleave and corrupt messages. The per-connection `asyncio.Lock` serializes all sends to a given client. The lock is per-connection, not per-canvas, so sends to different clients remain parallel.
512	
513	### 7g. Column Whitelisting for SQL Injection Prevention
514	
515	```python
516	# ws.py
517	_ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS = frozenset({
518	    "id", "type", "x", "y", "width", "height",
519	    "fill", "stroke", "stroke_width", "text", "font_size",
520	})
521	
522	def _shape_to_columns(shape: dict):
523	    for wire_key, value in shape.items():
524	        db_key = SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key, wire_key)
525	        if db_key not in _ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS:
526	            continue  # silently drop unknown keys
527	```
528	
529	Because shape data comes from the WebSocket (untrusted JSON), and column names are interpolated into SQL via f-strings (`INSERT INTO shapes ({col_names})`), a malicious client could inject SQL through crafted key names. The whitelist ensures only known column names are ever placed into queries. Values always go through asyncpg's parameterized `$N` placeholders.
530	
531	The same pattern applies to `_ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS` (which additionally excludes `id` and `type` since those are immutable).
532	
533	---
534	
535	## 8. Known Issues / Integration Notes
536	
537	### 8a. Error Envelope Handler in main.py
538	
539	```python
540	@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
541	async def http_exception_handler(request, exc):
542	    detail = exc.detail if isinstance(exc.detail, str) else "error"
543	    return JSONResponse(status_code=exc.status_code, content={"error": detail})
544	```
545	
546	This overrides FastAPI's default exception handler so ALL HTTPExceptions return `{"error": "<code>"}` instead of `{"detail": "<message>"}`. This is critical for the client's `ApiError` class, which reads `err.error` from the response body. Without this handler, auth failures would return `{"detail": "unauthorized"}` and the client would see `error: "unknown"`.
547	
548	Note that `auth.py` and `canvas.py` return `JSONResponse` directly for error cases (not raising HTTPException), so this handler only catches errors from `deps.py get_current_user()` and any unhandled FastAPI validation errors.
549	
550	### 8b. UUID String-to-Object Conversion in ws.py
551	
552	asyncpg requires `uuid.UUID` objects for UUID columns, not strings. The WebSocket handler receives shape IDs as strings from JSON and must convert them:
553	
554	```python
555	if db_key == "id" and isinstance(value, str):
556	    value = _uuid.UUID(value)
557	```
558	
559	This conversion happens in `_shape_to_columns()` for inserts, and explicitly in `_persist_update()` and `_persist_delete()` for WHERE clauses. Canvas ID and user ID are similarly converted at the top of `websocket_endpoint()`. Failure to convert would cause asyncpg `DataError`.
560	
561	### 8c. StrictMode Double-Mount Guard
562	
563	```typescript
564	// CanvasPage.tsx
565	const mountedRef = useRef(false);
566	
567	useEffect(() => {
568	    if (mountedRef.current) return;  // skip second mount in StrictMode
569	    mountedRef.current = true;
570	    // ... connect WS, load canvas ...
571	    return () => {
572	        mountedRef.current = false;
573	        ws.close();
574	    };
575	}, [canvasId]);
576	```
577	
578	React 19 StrictMode in development double-fires effects (mount -> unmount -> remount). Without this guard, two WebSocket connections would open simultaneously, causing duplicate messages and presence entries. The `mountedRef` flag ensures only the first mount actually connects.
579	
580	### 8d. HTTP/WS Init Race Condition Fix
581	
582	```typescript
583	// CanvasPage.tsx
584	const wsInitReceivedRef = useRef(false);
585	
586	// HTTP load (fallback):
587	getCanvasDetail(canvasId).then((detail) => {
588	    if (!wsInitReceivedRef.current) {
589	        shapesRef.current = detail.shapes;
590	    }
591	});
592	
593	// WS init (authoritative):
594	case "init":
595	    wsInitReceivedRef.current = true;
596	    shapesRef.current = msg.shapes;
597	```
598	
599	Both an HTTP GET and a WebSocket connection are initiated on mount. The HTTP response might arrive first with stale data, then the WS `init` arrives with the correct current state (including any ops that occurred between the HTTP response and WS connection). The `wsInitReceivedRef` flag ensures that if the WS init has already arrived, the HTTP response doesn't overwrite it with potentially stale data.
600	
601	### 8e. What's NOT Implemented
602	
603	| Feature | Status |
604	|---------|--------|
605	| **WebSocket reconnection** | Not implemented. If WS drops, user sees "Disconnected" but must manually navigate away and back. |
606	| **Ping/pong heartbeat** | Not implemented. Idle connections may be killed by proxies/firewalls without detection. |
607	| **Cursor interpolation (lerp)** | Not implemented. Remote cursors jump to new positions. No smoothing. |
608	| **Reconnect state recovery** | No incremental sync. A reconnect would need a fresh `init` (which happens naturally on remount). |
609	| **Rate limiting** | No connection or message rate limits on WS or HTTP. |
610	| **Input validation** | Minimal. WS messages with missing fields are silently ignored, but no schema validation. |
611	| **Text editing in-place** | Text creation uses `window.prompt()`. No in-canvas text editing overlay. |
612	| **Z-order manipulation** | Shapes are z-ordered by creation time (array index). No bring-to-front/send-to-back. |
613	| **Multi-select** | Only single shape selection. No box select or Shift+click. |
614	| **Pending ops divergence recovery** | If server ignores an op (no-op), the pendingOps set grows unboundedly until reconnect. |
615	| **seq counter persistence** | seq resets on server restart. Clients self-correct on reconnect via fresh `init`. |
616	| **Connection pool tuning for WS** | Pool is min=2, max=20. Each WS op holds a connection briefly. Could exhaust under load. |
617	| **Structured logging** | No logging anywhere in the backend. Errors are silently caught in WS handler. |
618	
619	---
620	
621	## 9. How to Run
622	
623	### Docker Compose (all services)
624	
625	```bash
626	docker compose up --build
627	```
628	
629	This starts:
630	- PostgreSQL on `:5432` (schema.sql runs on first boot via initdb.d mount)
631	- FastAPI server on `:3001` (waits for PG health check)
632	- Vite dev server on `:5173`
633	
634	Open `http://localhost:5173`.
635	
636	**Reset database** (if schema.sql changes):
637	```bash
638	docker compose down -v   # removes pgdata volume
639	docker compose up --build
640	```
641	
642	### Local Dev (DB in Docker, app native)
643	
644	```bash
645	# Terminal 1: Start just the database
646	docker compose up db
647	
648	# Terminal 2: Initialize schema and start backend
649	psql postgres://whiteboard:whiteboard@localhost:5432/whiteboard -f server/schema.sql
650	cd server
651	pip install -e .
652	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 3001
653	
654	# Terminal 3: Start frontend
655	cd client
656	npm install
657	npm run dev
658	```
659	
660	### Key Environment Variables
661	
662	| Variable | Used By | Default | Required |
663	|----------|---------|---------|----------|
664	| `DATABASE_URL` | server | `postgres://whiteboard:whiteboard@localhost:5432/whiteboard` | No |
665	| `JWT_SECRET` | server | `dev-secret-do-not-use-in-prod` | Yes (has fallback) |
666	| `VITE_WS_URL` | client (Docker only) | `ws://localhost:3001` | No |
667	| `VITE_API_URL` | client (Docker only) | `http://localhost:3001` | No |
668	
669	In local dev, the Vite proxy handles routing `/api` and `/ws` to `:3001`, so `VITE_*` vars are unnecessary. The client code constructs the WS URL from `window.location`:
670	
671	```typescript
672	const protocol = window.location.protocol === "https:" ? "wss:" : "ws:";
673	const host = window.location.host;
674	const wsUrl = `${protocol}//${host}/ws?canvasId=${canvasId}&token=${token}`;
675	```
676	
677	### Linting / Type Checking
678	
679	```bash
680	cd server && ruff check .        # Python linting
681	cd client && npx tsc --noEmit    # TypeScript type checking
682	```

1	"""
2	WebSocket handler for real-time whiteboard collaboration.
3	
4	Session-3 scope: server/app/ws.py only.
5	Implements: auth, init, op persistence+broadcast with seq/opId,
6	cursor relay, presence join/leave, per-canvas locking.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import asyncio
12	import json
13	import os
14	import uuid as _uuid
15	from collections import defaultdict
16	
17	from fastapi import WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect
18	
19	from app.db import get_pool
20	from app.types import (
21	    CURSOR_PALETTE,
22	    SHAPE_DB_TO_WIRE,
23	    SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB,
24	    AuthUser,
25	    PresenceUser,
26	)
27	
28	try:
29	    import jwt
30	except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover – pyjwt is a runtime dep
31	    raise
32	
33	# ── Module-level state (per-process, single worker) ─────────
34	
35	# canvas_id -> user_id -> {username, color, ws, send_lock}
36	presence: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)
37	
38	# canvas_id -> monotonically increasing int
39	seq_counters: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
40	
41	# canvas_id -> asyncio.Lock (serialises op processing per canvas)
42	canvas_locks: dict[str, asyncio.Lock] = defaultdict(asyncio.Lock)
43	
44	
45	# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
46	
47	
48	def _decode_token(token: str) -> AuthUser | None:
49	    """Decode a JWT token manually (no FastAPI DI for WebSocket)."""
50	    secret = os.environ.get("JWT_SECRET", "dev-secret-do-not-use-in-prod")
51	    if not secret:
52	        return None
53	    try:
54	        payload = jwt.decode(token, secret, algorithms=["HS256"])
55	    except (jwt.ExpiredSignatureError, jwt.InvalidTokenError):
56	        return None
57	    sub = payload.get("sub")
58	    username = payload.get("username")
59	    email = payload.get("email")
60	    if not sub or not username:
61	        return None
62	    return AuthUser(id=sub, username=username, email=email or "")
63	
64	
65	def _row_to_shape(row: dict) -> dict:
66	    """Convert an asyncpg Record (snake_case) to a wire-format Shape (camelCase)."""
67	    shape: dict = {}
68	    for key, value in row.items():
69	        if key in ("canvas_id",):
70	            continue  # internal, not on the wire
71	        # Omit None for optional fields (text, font_size) so the wire format
72	        # matches the NotRequired TypedDict contract.
73	        if value is None:
74	            continue
75	        wire_key = SHAPE_DB_TO_WIRE.get(key, key)
76	        # UUID -> str
77	        if hasattr(value, "hex"):
78	            shape[wire_key] = str(value)
79	        else:
80	            shape[wire_key] = value
81	    return shape
82	
83	
84	_ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS = frozenset({
85	    "id", "type", "x", "y", "width", "height",
86	    "fill", "stroke", "stroke_width", "text", "font_size",
87	})
88	
89	
90	def _shape_to_columns(shape: dict) -> tuple[list[str], list]:
91	    """Extract DB columns and values from a wire-format Shape dict for INSERT.
92	
93	    Only whitelisted column names are accepted to prevent SQL injection
94	    through crafted key names in the shape payload.
95	    """
96	    cols: list[str] = []
97	    vals: list = []
98	    for wire_key, value in shape.items():
99	        db_key = SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key, wire_key)
100	        if db_key not in _ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS:
101	            continue
102	        # asyncpg requires uuid.UUID for UUID columns
103	        if db_key == "id" and isinstance(value, str):
104	            value = _uuid.UUID(value)
105	        cols.append(db_key)
106	        vals.append(value)
107	    return cols, vals
108	
109	
110	async def _safe_send(ws: WebSocket, data: str, send_lock: asyncio.Lock) -> None:
111	    """Send text through a WebSocket, serialised per-connection to avoid frame interleaving."""
112	    try:
113	        async with send_lock:
114	            await ws.send_text(data)
115	    except Exception:
116	        pass  # connection already closed; disconnect handler will clean up
117	
118	
119	async def _broadcast(canvas_id: str, message: dict, *, exclude_user: str | None = None) -> None:
120	    """Broadcast a JSON message to all clients on a canvas.
121	
122	    Uses asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True so one slow/dead
123	    client never blocks the others (CLAUDE.md gotcha #2).
124	    """
125	    payload = json.dumps(message)
126	    targets = []
127	    for uid, info in presence.get(canvas_id, {}).items():
128	        if uid == exclude_user:
129	            continue
130	        targets.append(_safe_send(info["ws"], payload, info["send_lock"]))
131	    if targets:
132	        await asyncio.gather(*targets, return_exceptions=True)
133	
134	
135	async def _broadcast_all(canvas_id: str, message: dict) -> None:
136	    """Broadcast to ALL clients on a canvas, including the sender."""
137	    payload = json.dumps(message)
138	    targets = []
139	    for _uid, info in presence.get(canvas_id, {}).items():
140	        targets.append(_safe_send(info["ws"], payload, info["send_lock"]))
141	    if targets:
142	        await asyncio.gather(*targets, return_exceptions=True)
143	
144	
145	# ── Ping/Pong heartbeat ─────────────────────────────────────
146	
147	PING_INTERVAL = 30  # seconds between pings
148	PONG_TIMEOUT = 10   # seconds to wait for pong reply
149	
150	
151	async def _heartbeat_loop(
152	    ws: WebSocket,
153	    send_lock: asyncio.Lock,
154	    pong_event: asyncio.Event,
155	    cancel_event: asyncio.Event,
156	) -> None:
157	    """Send periodic pings and close the connection if pong is not received in time."""
158	    try:
159	        while not cancel_event.is_set():
160	            # Wait PING_INTERVAL seconds or until cancelled
161	            try:
162	                await asyncio.wait_for(cancel_event.wait(), timeout=PING_INTERVAL)
163	                return  # cancel_event was set
164	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
165	                pass  # time to ping
166	
167	            # Send ping
168	            pong_event.clear()
169	            await _safe_send(ws, json.dumps({"type": "ping"}), send_lock)
170	
171	            # Wait for pong
172	            try:
173	                await asyncio.wait_for(pong_event.wait(), timeout=PONG_TIMEOUT)
174	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
175	                # No pong received — close the connection
176	                try:
177	                    await ws.close(code=1000, reason="pong timeout")
178	                except Exception:
179	                    pass
180	                return
181	    except asyncio.CancelledError:
182	        pass
183	
184	
185	# ── Op persistence ───────────────────────────────────────────
186	
187	
188	async def _persist_add(conn, canvas_id: str, shape: dict) -> bool:
189	    """INSERT a new shape. Returns True if inserted, False if id already existed."""
190	    cols, vals = _shape_to_columns(shape)
191	    # Ensure canvas_id is included (as UUID for asyncpg)
192	    if "canvas_id" not in cols:
193	        cols.append("canvas_id")
194	        vals.append(_uuid.UUID(canvas_id) if isinstance(canvas_id, str) else canvas_id)
195	    placeholders = ", ".join(f"${i+1}" for i in range(len(cols)))
196	    col_names = ", ".join(cols)
197	    result = await conn.execute(
198	        f"INSERT INTO shapes ({col_names}) VALUES ({placeholders}) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING",
199	        *vals,
200	    )
201	    # asyncpg returns 'INSERT 0 1' or 'INSERT 0 0'
202	    return result.endswith("1")
203	
204	
205	_ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS = _ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS - {"id", "type"}
206	
207	
208	async def _persist_update(conn, canvas_id: str, shape_id: str, props: dict) -> bool:
209	    """UPDATE only the columns in props, scoped to this canvas.
210	
211	    Returns True if a row was modified.
212	    """
213	    if not props:
214	        return False
215	    set_parts: list[str] = []
216	    vals: list = []
217	    idx = 1
218	    for wire_key, value in props.items():
219	        db_key = SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key, wire_key)
220	        if db_key not in _ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS:
221	            continue
222	        set_parts.append(f"{db_key} = ${idx}")
223	        vals.append(value)
224	        idx += 1
225	    if not set_parts:
226	        return False
227	    vals.append(_uuid.UUID(shape_id) if isinstance(shape_id, str) else shape_id)
228	    vals.append(_uuid.UUID(canvas_id) if isinstance(canvas_id, str) else canvas_id)
229	    sql = f"UPDATE shapes SET {', '.join(set_parts)} WHERE id = ${idx} AND canvas_id = ${idx + 1}"
230	    result = await conn.execute(sql, *vals)
231	    # asyncpg returns 'UPDATE N'
232	    return not result.endswith("0")
233	
234	
235	async def _persist_delete(conn, canvas_id: str, shape_id: str) -> bool:
236	    """DELETE a shape, scoped to this canvas. Returns True if a row was removed."""
237	    result = await conn.execute(
238	        "DELETE FROM shapes WHERE id = $1 AND canvas_id = $2",
239	        _uuid.UUID(shape_id) if isinstance(shape_id, str) else shape_id,
240	        _uuid.UUID(canvas_id) if isinstance(canvas_id, str) else canvas_id,
241	    )
242	    return not result.endswith("0")
243	
244	
245	# ── Main endpoint ────────────────────────────────────────────
246	
247	
248	async def websocket_endpoint(ws: WebSocket) -> None:
249	    """WebSocket endpoint for a canvas session.
250	
251	    Connection: WS /ws?canvasId=<uuid>&token=<jwt>
252	    """
253	    # ── Auth before accept ───────────────────────────────────
254	    canvas_id = ws.query_params.get("canvasId", "")
255	    token = ws.query_params.get("token", "")
256	
257	    # Accept first to avoid Starlette/uvicorn issues with close-before-accept
258	    await ws.accept()
259	
260	    if not canvas_id or not token:
261	        await ws.close(code=4001, reason="missing canvasId or token")
262	        return
263	
264	    user = _decode_token(token)
265	    if user is None:
266	        await ws.close(code=4001, reason="auth failure")
267	        return
268	
269	    user_id: str = user["id"]
270	    username: str = user["username"]
271	
272	    # Verify canvas membership
273	    pool = await get_pool()
274	    try:
275	        cid = _uuid.UUID(canvas_id)
276	        uid = _uuid.UUID(user_id)
277	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
278	        await ws.close(code=4001, reason="invalid canvasId or userId")
279	        return
280	
281	    async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
282	        row = await conn.fetchrow(
283	            "SELECT 1 FROM canvas_members WHERE canvas_id = $1 AND user_id = $2",
284	            cid,
285	            uid,
286	        )
287	    if row is None:
288	        await ws.close(code=4003, reason="not a canvas member")
289	        return
290	
291	    # Per-connection send lock to prevent frame interleaving (CLAUDE.md gotcha #5)
292	    send_lock = asyncio.Lock()
293	
294	    # Heartbeat events
295	    pong_event = asyncio.Event()
296	    cancel_heartbeat = asyncio.Event()
297	
298	    # ── Presence ─────────────────────────────────────────────
299	    canvas_presence = presence[canvas_id]
300	    color_index = len(canvas_presence) % len(CURSOR_PALETTE)
301	    color = CURSOR_PALETTE[color_index]
302	
303	    me: PresenceUser = PresenceUser(
304	        userId=user_id,
305	        username=username,
306	        color=color,
307	    )
308	    canvas_presence[user_id] = {
309	        "username": username,
310	        "color": color,
311	        "ws": ws,
312	        "send_lock": send_lock,
313	    }
314	
315	    try:
316	        # ── Send init ────────────────────────────────────────
317	        async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
318	            rows = await conn.fetch(
319	                "SELECT * FROM shapes WHERE canvas_id = $1 ORDER BY id",
320	                cid,
321	            )
322	        shapes = [_row_to_shape(dict(r)) for r in rows]
323	        users = [
324	            PresenceUser(userId=uid, username=info["username"], color=info["color"])
325	            for uid, info in canvas_presence.items()
326	        ]
327	        current_seq = seq_counters[canvas_id]
328	
329	        init_msg = json.dumps({
330	            "type": "init",
331	            "shapes": shapes,
332	            "users": users,
333	            "seq": current_seq,
334	        })
335	        async with send_lock:
336	            await ws.send_text(init_msg)
337	
338	        # Broadcast join to others
339	        await _broadcast(canvas_id, {"type": "join", "user": dict(me)}, exclude_user=user_id)
340	
341	        # Start heartbeat task
342	        heartbeat_task = asyncio.create_task(
343	            _heartbeat_loop(ws, send_lock, pong_event, cancel_heartbeat)
344	        )
345	
346	        # ── Message loop ─────────────────────────────────────
347	        while True:
348	            raw = await ws.receive_text()
349	            try:
350	                msg = json.loads(raw)
351	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
352	                continue  # ignore malformed messages
353	
354	            msg_type = msg.get("type")
355	
356	            if msg_type == "pong":
357	                pong_event.set()
358	                continue
359	
360	            if msg_type == "op":
361	                op = msg.get("op")
362	                op_id = msg.get("opId", "")
363	                if not op or not isinstance(op, dict):
364	                    continue
365	
366	                kind = op.get("kind")
367	                lock = canvas_locks[canvas_id]
368	
369	                # Acquire per-canvas lock, persist, assign seq, then
370	                # release before broadcast (contract steps 2-7).
371	                modified = False
372	                seq = 0
373	                async with lock:
374	                    async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
375	                        if kind == "add":
376	                            shape = op.get("shape")
377	                            if shape and isinstance(shape, dict) and "id" in shape:
378	                                modified = await _persist_add(conn, canvas_id, shape)
379	                        elif kind == "update":
380	                            shape_id = op.get("shapeId")
381	                            props = op.get("props")
382	                            if shape_id and props and isinstance(props, dict):
383	                                modified = await _persist_update(conn, canvas_id, shape_id, props)
384	                        elif kind == "delete":
385	                            shape_id = op.get("shapeId")
386	                            if shape_id:
387	                                modified = await _persist_delete(conn, canvas_id, shape_id)
388	                    if modified:
389	                        seq_counters[canvas_id] += 1
390	                        seq = seq_counters[canvas_id]
391	                # Lock released — now broadcast outside the lock
392	                if modified:
393	                    await _broadcast_all(canvas_id, {
394	                        "type": "op",
395	                        "op": op,
396	                        "userId": user_id,
397	                        "seq": seq,
398	                        "opId": op_id,
399	                    })
400	                # If not modified (no-op): no broadcast, no seq increment
401	
402	            elif msg_type == "cursor":
403	                x = msg.get("x")
404	                y = msg.get("y")
405	                if x is not None and y is not None:
406	                    await _broadcast(canvas_id, {
407	                        "type": "cursor",
408	                        "userId": user_id,
409	                        "username": username,
410	                        "x": x,
411	                        "y": y,
412	                    }, exclude_user=user_id)
413	
414	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
415	        pass
416	    except Exception as exc:
417	        # Send error before disconnecting so client knows what happened
418	        try:
419	            async with send_lock:
420	                await ws.send_text(json.dumps({
421	                    "type": "error",
422	                    "message": "internal server error",
423	                }))
424	        except Exception:
425	            pass
426	    finally:
427	        # ── Cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────────
428	        cancel_heartbeat.set()
429	        try:
430	            heartbeat_task.cancel()
431	            await heartbeat_task
432	        except Exception:
433	            pass
434	
435	        canvas_presence.pop(user_id, None)
436	
437	        # Broadcast leave to remaining clients
438	        await _broadcast(canvas_id, {"type": "leave", "userId": user_id})
439	
440	        # Do NOT delete canvas_locks/seq_counters — avoids race on
441	        # simultaneous disconnect and prevents seq reset mid-session.

## 8. Known Issues / Integration Notes
input.new_string:
The same pattern applies to `_ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS` (which additionally excludes `id` and `type` since those are immutable).

### 7h. Floating Bottom Toolbar with SVG Icons and Color Palette

The toolbar is a dark (`#2c2c2c`) floating bar, fixed at bottom-center with `position: fixed`. Tool buttons use inline SVG path icons (not icon fonts or images). Fill and stroke each expose an 8-color `PALETTE` as swatch buttons plus a native `<input type="color">` picker behind a "+" affordance. The back button is separated from the bar and placed top-left. Groups (tools, colors, undo/redo) are visually separated by 1px dividers.

### 7i. Dot-Grid Background and Z-Order Rendering

`canvasRenderer.ts` draws a dot grid (1px dots at 20px spacing, color `#d4d5db`) over the `#f8f9fb` background on every frame. Shapes are drawn in array order (creation order), except the currently selected shape is skipped on the first pass and drawn last -- this gives the selected shape a visual "lift" above all others without mutating the shapes array. Preview shapes (during draw-drag) render after the selected shape.

### 7j. WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff

```typescript
// CanvasPage.tsx
const MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS = 10;
const BACKOFF_DELAYS = [1000, 2000, 4000, 8000, 16000, 30000]; // cap at 30s
// Jitter: delay * (0.5 + Math.random() * 0.5)
```

On unexpected WS close, the client retries up to 10 times with capped exponential backoff plus random jitter. Auth failures (close codes `4001`, `4003`) skip reconnection entirely. After max attempts, a red banner appears: "Connection lost. Click to retry." which resets the counter. On successful reconnect, the server sends a fresh `init` message, which resets shapes, presence, undo/redo stacks, and pending ops. Connection status is displayed as a colored pill (green/yellow/red) in the bottom-left corner with three states: `connected`, `reconnecting`, `disconnected`.

### 7k. Server-Side Ping/Pong Heartbeat

```python
# ws.py
PING_INTERVAL = 30  # seconds between pings
PONG_TIMEOUT = 10   # seconds to wait for pong reply
```

The server runs a `_heartbeat_loop` asyncio task per connection. Every 30 seconds it sends `{"type": "ping"}` over the WebSocket. If no `{"type": "pong"}` is received within 10 seconds, the server closes the connection with code `1000` ("pong timeout"). The heartbeat task is cancelled via a `cancel_event` in the connection's `finally` block. The client handles `ping` messages before casting to the typed `ServerMessage` union (since `ping` is not in the union type).

### 7l. Cursor Lerp Interpolation and Opacity Fade

```typescript
// CanvasPage.tsx cursor lerp loop
const LERP_FACTOR = 0.15;
const FADE_AFTER_MS = 5000;
const FADED_OPACITY = 0.3;
```

Remote cursor positions are stored as *targets* in a separate `cursorTargetsRef` map. A dedicated `requestAnimationFrame` loop runs continuously, interpolating each cursor's displayed position toward its target at a factor of 0.15 per frame. Cursors snap to the target when within 0.1px. After 5 seconds without a new cursor message, the cursor's opacity fades to 0.3 (rendered via `ctx.globalAlpha` in `drawCursor()`). The `CursorInfo` type includes an optional `opacity` field for this.

### 8e. What's NOT Implemented

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| **WebSocket reconnection** | Not implemented. If WS drops, user sees "Disconnected" but must manually navigate away and back. |
| **Ping/pong heartbeat** | Not implemented. Idle connections may be killed by proxies/firewalls without detection. |
| **Cursor interpolation (lerp)** | Not implemented. Remote cursors jump to new positions. No smoothing. |
| **Reconnect state recovery** | No incremental sync. A reconnect would need a fresh `init` (which happens naturally on remount). |
| **Rate limiting** | No connection or message rate limits on WS or HTTP. |
| **Input validation** | Minimal. WS messages with missing fields are silently ignored, but no schema validation. |
| **Text editing in-place** | Text creation uses `window.prompt()`. No in-canvas text editing overlay. |
| **Z-order manipulation** | Shapes are z-ordered by creation time (array index). No bring-to-front/send-to-back. |
| **Multi-select** | Only single shape selection. No box select or Shift+click. |
| **Pending ops divergence recovery** | If server ignores an op (no-op), the pendingOps set grows unboundedly until reconnect. |
| **seq counter persistence** | seq resets on server restart. Clients self-correct on reconnect via fresh `init`. |
| **Connection pool tuning for WS** | Pool is min=2, max=20. Each WS op holds a connection briefly. Could exhaust under load. |
| **Structured logging** | No logging anywhere in the backend. Errors are silently caught in WS handler. |

content.text: Now update the StrictMode guard note (8c) to mention that the guard also prevents duplicate reconnection timers.

input.old_string: React 19 StrictMode in development double-fires effects (mount -> unmount -> remount). Without this guard, two WebSocket connections would open simultaneously, causing duplicate messages and presence entries. The `mountedRef` flag ensures only the first mount actually connects.
input.new_string: React 19 StrictMode in development double-fires effects (mount -> unmount -> remount). Without this guard, two WebSocket connections would open simultaneously, causing duplicate messages, presence entries, and duplicate reconnection timers. The `mountedRef` flag ensures only the first mount actually connects. The cleanup function sets `mountedRef = false`, clears `shouldReconnectRef`, cancels any pending reconnect timer, and closes the WebSocket.

520	### 7g. Column Whitelisting for SQL Injection Prevention
521	
522	```python
523	# ws.py
524	_ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS = frozenset({
525	    "id", "type", "x", "y", "width", "height",
526	    "fill", "stroke", "stroke_width", "text", "font_size",
527	})
528	
529	def _shape_to_columns(shape: dict):
530	    for wire_key, value in shape.items():
531	        db_key = SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key, wire_key)
532	        if db_key not in _ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS:
533	            continue  # silently drop unknown keys
534	```
535	
536	Because shape data comes from the WebSocket (untrusted JSON), and column names are interpolated into SQL via f-strings (`INSERT INTO shapes ({col_names})`), a malicious client could inject SQL through crafted key names. The whitelist ensures only known column names are ever placed into queries. Values always go through asyncpg's parameterized `$N` placeholders.
537	
538	The same pattern applies to `_ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS` (which additionally excludes `id` and `type` since those are immutable).
539	
540	### 7h. Floating Bottom Toolbar with SVG Icons and Color Palette
541	
542	The toolbar is a dark (`#2c2c2c`) floating bar, fixed at bottom-center with `position: fixed`. Tool buttons use inline SVG path icons (not icon fonts or images). Fill and stroke each expose an 8-color `PALETTE` as swatch buttons plus a native `<input type="color">` picker behind a "+" affordance. The back button is separated from the bar and placed top-left. Groups (tools, colors, undo/redo) are visually separated by 1px dividers.
543	
544	### 7i. Dot-Grid Background and Z-Order Rendering
545	
546	`canvasRenderer.ts` draws a dot grid (1px dots at 20px spacing, color `#d4d5db`) over the `#f8f9fb` background on every frame. Shapes are drawn in array order (creation order), except the currently selected shape is skipped on the first pass and drawn last -- this gives the selected shape a visual "lift" above all others without mutating the shapes array. Preview shapes (during draw-drag) render after the selected shape.
547	
548	### 7j. WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff
549	
550	```typescript
551	// CanvasPage.tsx
552	const MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS = 10;
553	const BACKOFF_DELAYS = [1000, 2000, 4000, 8000, 16000, 30000]; // cap at 30s
554	// Jitter: delay * (0.5 + Math.random() * 0.5)
555	```
556	
557	On unexpected WS close, the client retries up to 10 times with capped exponential backoff plus random jitter. Auth failures (close codes `4001`, `4003`) skip reconnection entirely. After max attempts, a red banner appears: "Connection lost. Click to retry." which resets the counter. On successful reconnect, the server sends a fresh `init` message, which resets shapes, presence, undo/redo stacks, and pending ops. Connection status is displayed as a colored pill (green/yellow/red) in the bottom-left corner with three states: `connected`, `reconnecting`, `disconnected`.
558	
559	### 7k. Server-Side Ping/Pong Heartbeat
560	
561	```python
562	# ws.py
563	PING_INTERVAL = 30  # seconds between pings
564	PONG_TIMEOUT = 10   # seconds to wait for pong reply
565	```
566	
567	The server runs a `_heartbeat_loop` asyncio task per connection. Every 30 seconds it sends `{"type": "ping"}` over the WebSocket. If no `{"type": "pong"}` is received within 10 seconds, the server closes the connection with code `1000` ("pong timeout"). The heartbeat task is cancelled via a `cancel_event` in the connection's `finally` block. The client handles `ping` messages before casting to the typed `ServerMessage` union (since `ping` is not in the union type).
568	
569	### 7l. Cursor Lerp Interpolation and Opacity Fade
570	
571	```typescript
572	// CanvasPage.tsx cursor lerp loop
573	const LERP_FACTOR = 0.15;
574	const FADE_AFTER_MS = 5000;
575	const FADED_OPACITY = 0.3;
576	```
577	
578	Remote cursor positions are stored as *targets* in a separate `cursorTargetsRef` map. A dedicated `requestAnimationFrame` loop runs continuously, interpolating each cursor's displayed position toward its target at a factor of 0.15 per frame. Cursors snap to the target when within 0.1px. After 5 seconds without a new cursor message, the cursor's opacity fades to 0.3 (rendered via `ctx.globalAlpha` in `drawCursor()`). The `CursorInfo` type includes an optional `opacity` field for this.
579	
580	---
581	
582	## 8. Known Issues / Integration Notes
583	
584	### 8a. Error Envelope Handler in main.py
585	
586	```python
587	@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
588	async def http_exception_handler(request, exc):
589	    detail = exc.detail if isinstance(exc.detail, str) else "error"
590	    return JSONResponse(status_code=exc.status_code, content={"error": detail})
591	```
592	
593	This overrides FastAPI's default exception handler so ALL HTTPExceptions return `{"error": "<code>"}` instead of `{"detail": "<message>"}`. This is critical for the client's `ApiError` class, which reads `err.error` from the response body. Without this handler, auth failures would return `{"detail": "unauthorized"}` and the client would see `error: "unknown"`.
594	
595	Note that `auth.py` and `canvas.py` return `JSONResponse` directly for error cases (not raising HTTPException), so this handler only catches errors from `deps.py get_current_user()` and any unhandled FastAPI validation errors.
596	
597	### 8b. UUID String-to-Object Conversion in ws.py
598	
599	asyncpg requires `uuid.UUID` objects for UUID columns, not strings. The WebSocket handler receives shape IDs as strings from JSON and must convert them:
600	
601	```python
602	if db_key == "id" and isinstance(value, str):
603	    value = _uuid.UUID(value)
604	```
605	
606	This conversion happens in `_shape_to_columns()` for inserts, and explicitly in `_persist_update()` and `_persist_delete()` for WHERE clauses. Canvas ID and user ID are similarly converted at the top of `websocket_endpoint()`. Failure to convert would cause asyncpg `DataError`.
607

624	
625	React 19 StrictMode in development double-fires effects (mount -> unmount -> remount). Without this guard, two WebSocket connections would open simultaneously, causing duplicate messages, presence entries, and duplicate reconnection timers. The `mountedRef` flag ensures only the first mount actually connects. The cleanup function sets `mountedRef = false`, clears `shouldReconnectRef`, cancels any pending reconnect timer, and closes the WebSocket.
626	
627	### 8d. HTTP/WS Init Race Condition Fix
628	
629	```typescript
630	// CanvasPage.tsx
631	const wsInitReceivedRef = useRef(false);
632	
633	// HTTP load (fallback):
634	getCanvasDetail(canvasId).then((detail) => {
635	    if (!wsInitReceivedRef.current) {
636	        shapesRef.current = detail.shapes;
637	    }
638	});
639	
640	// WS init (authoritative):
641	case "init":
642	    wsInitReceivedRef.current = true;
643	    shapesRef.current = msg.shapes;
644	```
645	
646	Both an HTTP GET and a WebSocket connection are initiated on mount. The HTTP response might arrive first with stale data, then the WS `init` arrives with the correct current state (including any ops that occurred between the HTTP response and WS connection). The `wsInitReceivedRef` flag ensures that if the WS init has already arrived, the HTTP response doesn't overwrite it with potentially stale data.
647	
648	### 8e. Input Focus Guard on Keyboard Shortcuts
649	
650	```typescript
651	// CanvasPage.tsx handleKeyDown():
652	const tag = (e.target as HTMLElement)?.tagName;
653	if (tag === "INPUT" || tag === "TEXTAREA" || (e.target as HTMLElement)?.isContentEditable) return;
654	```
655	
656	Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Shift+Z, Delete/Backspace) are skipped when focus is on an input, textarea, or contentEditable element. This prevents undo/delete from firing while the user types in the invite panel or canvas name input.
657	
658	### 8f. What's NOT Implemented
659	
660	| Feature | Status |
661	|---------|--------|
662	| **WebSocket reconnection** | Implemented. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (up to 10 attempts, max 30s delay with jitter). Manual retry button after exhaustion. See 7j. |
663	| **Ping/pong heartbeat** | Implemented. Server pings every 30s, closes connection if no pong within 10s. See 7k. |
664	| **Cursor interpolation (lerp)** | Implemented. rAF loop interpolates at factor 0.15 per frame; opacity fades to 0.3 after 5s idle. See 7l. |
665	| **Reconnect state recovery** | No incremental sync. Reconnect triggers a fresh `init` from the server, resetting shapes, presence, undo/redo stacks, and pending ops. |
666	| **Rate limiting** | No connection or message rate limits on WS or HTTP. |
667	| **Input validation** | Minimal. WS messages with missing fields are silently ignored, but no schema validation. |
668	| **Text editing in-place** | Text creation uses `window.prompt()`. No in-canvas text editing overlay. |
669	| **Z-order manipulation** | Shapes are z-ordered by creation time (array index). Selected shape is drawn last for visual lift but array order is not mutated. No bring-to-front/send-to-back user action. |
670	| **Multi-select** | Only single shape selection. No box select or Shift+click. |
671	| **Pending ops divergence recovery** | If server ignores an op (no-op), the pendingOps set grows unboundedly until reconnect. |
672	| **seq counter persistence** | seq resets on server restart. Clients self-correct on reconnect via fresh `init`. |
673	| **Connection pool tuning for WS** | Pool is min=2, max=20. Each WS op holds a connection briefly. Could exhaust under load. |
674	| **Structured logging** | No logging anywhere in the backend. Errors are silently caught in WS handler. |
